title: papaver alpinum var. endressii
intro: Papaver alpinum var. endressii, also known as Himalayan poppy or Endress's alpine poppy, is a perennial plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to the Himalayas and other mountainous regions. The plant contains alkaloids, including reticuline, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, such as analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential remedy for pain relief and inflammation. The health benefits of Papaver alpinum var. endressii include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its possible use in the treatment of certain skin conditions. The plant can be used to prepare herbal remedies, such as tinctures and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine practices.
